title: PowerShell Navigation & Shell Basics
description: Learn to navigate the filesystem, inspect locations, manage the directory stack, and use shell history in PowerShell.

tasks.prompt: Display the current working directory path.
tasks.hint: Use Get-Location or the pwd alias.
tasks.explanation: Get-Location returns the current path in the PowerShell session.

tasks.hint: Use Set-Location or cd with the full path.
tasks.explanation: Set-Location changes the current working directory for subsequent commands.

tasks.prompt: List all files and folders in the current directory.
tasks.hint: Use Get-ChildItem or the dir/ls aliases.
tasks.explanation: Get-ChildItem lists items in the current or specified path.

tasks.prompt: List all items in 'C:\Windows\Logs' recursively, including subdirectories.
tasks.explanation: The -Recurse switch walks all nested directories under the target path.

tasks.prompt: Push 'C:\Temp' onto the location stack (save current path and navigate there).
tasks.hint: Use Push-Location or the pushd alias.
tasks.explanation: Push-Location saves the current location and moves to a new path on the stack.

tasks.prompt: Pop the most recent entry off the location stack and return to the previous directory.
tasks.hint: Use Pop-Location or the popd alias.
tasks.explanation: Pop-Location restores the directory saved by the last Push-Location.

tasks.hint: Use Clear-Host or the cls alias.
tasks.explanation: Clear-Host removes visible output from the console buffer.

tasks.prompt: Display the command history for the current PowerShell session.
tasks.hint: Use Get-History or the h alias.
tasks.explanation: Get-History lists commands executed during the current session.
